what_it_does: A deterministic, read-only validator for code references stored in register.json (features[].code) and addresses.json (addresses.*.code). It resolves each reference against the source tree under ~/company, classifies it as ok, drift, or unverifiable, records a repair worklist in refcheck-report.json, and also exposes a separate deterministic floor (check_dossier) for proposed registry dossiers that checks capabilities against a closed vocabulary, maps_to_feature against known feature ids, and optional code refs against real files or symbols.
observations: Reads two registries (register.json and addresses.json) and fails loudly if either is malformed.
Handles compound refs by splitting on ' / ' or ' + ' and validates each sub-ref independently.
Recognises several ref forms: file:line paths, /api/... routes, Suite.verb symbols, bare symbols, and unverifiable globs or selectors.
Uses regex to extract nearest Python def/class and TSX-style declarations for line-based refs.
Computes a git snapshot of ~/company so line numbers are interpretable against a known revision.
Imports contracts.ui_info to reuse a closed capability vocabulary for dossier validation.
Provides a single dispatch helper (_check_one_ref) used by both the corpus run and the dossier floor.
Never repairs refs; it only emits a report and printed summary for a lead to act on.
standouts: The check_dossier function adds a no-model fabrication gate for proposed registry entries.
Relocation detection tries to name a concrete repair target when a candidate symbol is defined elsewhere in the same file.
An already-correct guard prevents a secondary label token from overriding a ref that already sits on a matching symbol.
Unverifiable refs are classified honestly rather than forced into drift or ok.
The same ref-resolution dispatch is reused for corpus drift and dossier code checks.
conventions: Docstrings explain usage, layer role, and conservative verdict order.
Private helpers are prefixed with underscore; module-level constants are uppercase.
Regexes are compiled once at module load time.
Malformed registry data raises ValueError rather than being silently skipped.
Compound references are split with explicit surrounding spaces to avoid splitting path slashes.
concerns: Fabrication detection in check_dossier relies on substring matches inside free-form points_at strings.
SEARCH_DIRS and COMPANY are hardcoded, so source layout changes require editing the script.
A ref with a non-existent path but no file extension is classified unverifiable, not fabrication.
The relatedness logic in _candidate_match is complex and may over- or under-match symbol tokens.
Some file opens are not wrapped in with statements, though they are short-lived.
The promised model-based semantic layer is only described in comments; it is not implemented here.
notes: A read-only deterministic auditor that checks whether registry code refs still point at real source symbols and whether proposed dossier entries stay inside the closed capability and feature vocabulary.
questions: What is the exact schema of register.json and addresses.json beyond the fields read here?
Where is bridge.py expected to reside relative to the hardcoded SEARCH_DIRS?
What guarantees does contracts/ui_info provide for normalize_capabilities and the capability map?
How are downstream consumers expected to handle unverifiable refs?
What build or cron process updates ~/company, and how often should this script be rerun?
What do the project-specific terms RG3, RG6, and register_element role refer to?
Will the future model layer read refcheck-report.json or call these functions directly?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file both validates code-reference drift against the real source and implements the deterministic dossier no-fiction floor described in its docstring.
apparent_themes: Traceability between design corpus and implementation
Conservative classification with explicit repair signals
Human repair worklist rather than automated repair
intention_signals: The file is trying to keep a growing design corpus honest as the codebase changes, giving maintainers concrete repair targets while preventing fabricated capabilities, features, or nonexistent code refs from entering the registry through a deterministic gate.
novelty: It combines line-level code drift detection with a closed-vocabulary dossier fabrication check in one script, and uses candidate-symbol matching to suggest where a drifted reference should move.
contribution: A deterministic, read-only validation pass that emits a drift repair worklist and a no-fiction dossier gate, usable by both corpus-wide ref checks and per-dossier confirmation.
summary_for_embedding: refcheck.py is a deterministic Python script that audits code: references in register.json and addresses.json against the ~/company source tree, detects drift, suggests repair targets, and provides a no-model fabrication floor for proposed registry dossiers.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to keep design corpus code references traceable as the implementation changes and to prevent fabricated capabilities, features, or code refs from entering the registry without a deterministic check.

symbols.does: Splits a compound code reference into independent sub-refs.
symbols.description: Uses a regex to split on ' / ' or ' + ' boundaries, returning a list of trimmed sub-refs so each can be validated separately.

symbols.does: Extracts a leading file:line component from a sub-ref.
symbols.description: Matches a file-with-extension head and an optional line number, and also recovers a trailing :N from the remainder when the head did not capture it.

symbols.does: Resolves a relative or repo-relative source path under ~/company.
symbols.description: Searches a fixed set of code directories and then the company root, returning the repo-relative path and file lines, or None if the file is not found.

symbols.does: Finds the nearest preceding def/class or TSX declaration above a given line.
symbols.description: Walks upward from a zero-based line index, matching Python or TSX-style declaration regexes, and returns the symbol name, its one-based line, and the distance.

symbols.does: Collects every def/class or TSX declaration in a file.
symbols.description: Scans all lines and records the first occurrence line for each symbol name, producing a map used for relocation checks.

symbols.does: Determines whether a candidate symbol names a definition elsewhere in the file.
symbols.description: Compares identifier-shaped candidates against the file's definition map, guards against already-correct cases, and returns the best alternative target ranked by exact match and proximity to the original line.

symbols.does: Local ranking key for candidate matches.
symbols.description: Prefers exact symbol matches and, when a reference line is known, chooses the definition closest to that line.

symbols.does: Checks whether an /api/... route literal appears in bridge.py.
symbols.description: Scans the provided bridge.py lines for the exact route string and returns ok or drift accordingly.

symbols.does: Checks whether a Suite.verb or bare symbol is present in source lines.
symbols.description: Uses the last dotted segment as the symbol name and searches for a def/class or bare occurrence in the given lines.

symbols.does: Validates a single file:line sub-ref against resolved source.
symbols.description: Resolves the file, checks line existence and proximity to a definition, detects relocation to a candidate-defined symbol elsewhere, and returns a conservative ok/drift/unverifiable verdict.

symbols.does: Derives plausible symbol names from an owner id and label.
symbols.description: Takes the suffix after the last dash in the owner id and extracts snake_case or camelCase identifiers from the label, filtering out short or non-identifier tokens.

symbols.does: Gathers every code ref from register.json and addresses.json.
symbols.description: Iterates features[].code and addresses.*.code, building records with raw ref, source registry, owner, and label, raising on malformed input.

symbols.does: Dispatches one sub-ref to the appropriate form checker.
symbols.description: Routes /api/ refs, globs, Suite.verb symbols, bare symbols, and file:line refs to the corresponding checker so the same logic is reused by run_corpus and check_dossier.

symbols.does: Loads the set of known feature ids from register.json.
symbols.description: Reads register.json, validates that features is a list, and returns the set of feature ids used to validate dossier maps_to_feature values.

symbols.does: Deterministic no-fiction floor for one proposed registry dossier.
symbols.description: Verifies that capabilities belong to the closed vocabulary, maps_to_feature is a known feature id or 'proposed', and any code ref resolves to a real file or symbol rather than a fabricated one.

symbols.does: Runs the full corpus drift validation and writes the report.
symbols.description: Loads the registries, resolves every sub-ref through _check_one_ref, captures a git snapshot, writes refcheck-report.json, and prints a summary with drift details.
Simple wrapper invoked when the script is run directly.
